meta_title: For Students: SaaS pricing to Solve no project ideas (Intermediate Comparison)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS pricing and overcome no project ideas. A Intermediate-level Comparison using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: SaaS Pricing to Solve No Project Ideas
content.seo_description: Learn to implement SaaS pricing to overcome project stagnation. An intermediate guide using Antigravity and Claude Code to build your first real-world app.
content.answer_block: Stop searching for the 'perfect' idea and build a pricing engine. By architecting a tiered subscription model for a niche utility, you solve the 'no project' paralysis. Use Antigravity for rapid scaffolding and Claude Code to automate the integration, turning a blank screen into a functional, monetizable SaaS prototype.
content.intro:
The screen is a void. You have the technical skills, but the paralysis of 'what to build' is stalling your career growth. The secret isn't a revolutionary idea; it's the mastery of the business logic that separates a hobbyist from a SaaS engineer.

By building a robust pricing architecture, you move from a student writing scripts to a developer engineering value. We will use Antigravity to scaffold your infrastructure and Claude Code to accelerate the implementation, transforming your portfolio into a professional-grade asset that hiring managers actually want to see.

steps.title: Define the Tiered Value Proposition
steps.description: Identify three distinct user personas (Free, Pro, Enterprise) and map specific feature gates to each tier.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a database schema and TypeScript interfaces for a SaaS pricing model with three tiers: Free (rate-limited), Pro (unlimited), and Enterprise (SSO/Custom). Include usage-based billing logic.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Create a pricing.ts utility to handle tier validation logic based on the schema generated by Antigravity.'

steps.title: Implement Stripe Subscription Logic
steps.description: Connect your backend to Stripe's API to handle checkout sessions and webhook events.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a Stripe service class that handles creating checkout sessions and verifying subscription status via webhooks for a Next.js backend.
steps.claude_code_command: claude terminal --execute 'npm install stripe && npx ts-node setup-stripe-webhooks.ts'

steps.title: Build Dynamic Feature Gating
steps.description: Create a middleware or component wrapper that hides premium UI elements based on the user's active subscription status.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a React Higher-Order Component (HOC) called 'withSubscriptionGuard' that checks user metadata against an API endpoint and redirects unauthorized users.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Refactor my dashboard components to use the withSubscriptionGuard HOC for all premium features.'

steps.title: Integrate Usage Analytics
steps.description: Track how users interact with your tiers to iterate on your pricing strategy.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a middleware that logs user API calls and associates them with their subscription tier in a PostgreSQL database.
steps.claude_code_command: claude write --file middleware/usage-tracker.ts --content 'Implement logging for tier-based API consumption.'

steps.title: Deploy and Validate Webhooks
steps.description: Expose your local development environment to the public internet to test Stripe webhooks in real-time.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a deployment configuration file for Vercel that includes environment variables for Stripe and database connections.

pro_tips: Always build your pricing logic as a decoupled service; it makes migrating from Stripe to Paddle or LemonSqueezy trivial later.
Use 'Feature Flags' instead of hard-coding tier checks to allow for instant A/B testing on pricing models.
Never trust the client-side for permission checks; always validate subscription status on the server-side before serving data.
Implement 'Grace Periods' in your webhook logic to handle payment failures without immediately locking out paying users.
Build a 'Pricing Dashboard' for yourself as an admin to manually override user tiers for testing or customer support.
content.next_steps: Once your pricing engine is live, deploy your project to a public URL and share it on the AI Builder Discord to gather user feedback on your tiers.
content.conversion_block: You are no longer just a student; you are a SaaS architect capable of turning code into revenue. Don't let your project sit in a private repo. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
